objetivo: Consequently, this study aims to identify the applications and variables associated with QC-AI integration.

This study aims to systematically examine the intersection of quantum computing and artificial intelligence by identifying the key technological features, integration requirements, and sectoral applications that define the current state of the field.
This study aims to identify QC applications and methodologies applied to AI and to assess their impact on process optimization and complex problem-solving.
problema: Despite significant progress, AI-QC integration faces critical limitations: dependence on NISQ hardware with high error rates, lack of standardized methodologies, and insufficient conceptual frameworks for articulating applications and technical requirements (Ghodke et al., 2024).
However, these advantages are problem-specific and have been rigorously proven only for certain algorithms, such as Shor’s and Grover’s (Moret-Bonillo, 2015).
However, the lack of structured knowledge regarding specific QC methodologies and applications in AI hinders its optimal implementation and development.

The study concludes that although QC demonstrates substantial potential to enhance AI, its broader adoption remains constrained by reliance on NISQ hardware, the need for effective error correction, and the limited scalability of hybrid quantum classical algorithms.
metodo: To this end, a systematic literature review was conducted following the PRISMA 2020 methodology, drawing on studies from Scopus and Web of Science databases.

According to PRISMA 2020 is employed to identify and analyze applications and methodologies at the intersection of QC and AI, an interdisciplinary field that demands rigorous approaches to integrate high-quality and relevant studies.

contribuicoes: The review contributes by mapping existing research, highlighting methodological approaches, and revealing gaps that may guide targeted advancements in hybrid quantum AI systems.
These contributions aim to guide future experimental validations and hybrid model development.
The insights generated have the potential to accelerate innovation in high-impact domains such as healthcare, finance, energy, and cybersecurity.

Additionally, this review introduces an integrative framework that categorizes key AI QC convergence dimensions and proposes a classification of application areas based on technical requirements and algorithmic capabilities.

The contributions are threefold: (i) providing a structured synthesis and classification of literature on QC-AI inte gration under NISQ constraints, (ii) presenting sector-based examples that illustrate opportunities and use cases, and (iii) outlining a forwardlooking research agenda to guide the use of QC in enhancing AI.

objetivo: To bridge this gap, this paper proposes a quantum recurrent neural network (QRNN) designed specifically for sequential labeling tasks.
To overcome this research gap, we propose a hybrid quantum-classical recurrent neural network (QRNN) that is used specifically for sequential labeling tasks.
The proposed QRNN is a hybrid quantum-classical model, which combines quantum and classical resources.
problema: However, the superiority of transformer-based models poses various challenges, such as demands for a large amount of data and parameters to train models [4].
Quantum computing can efficiently solve problems that are difficult for traditional computing, such as factoring large numbers [7].

Although several studies have explored the application of QML techniques to NLP such as language modeling [17], text classification [18], and sentiment analysis [19], there is still a noticeable research gap in applying quantum-enhanced architectures to sequential labeling, a core problem in NLP that involves assigning categorical labels to each token in a sequence.
However, these algorithms cannot be applied to current quantum hardware due to their dependence on quantum RAM to execute.

QRNN is applied to perform POS tagging in nine different code-mixed datasets, where each dataset represents a unique combination of languages.
contribuicoes: Our main contribution is: 1. to develop a QRNN that combines VQCs with classical layers to perform efficient sequential labeling; 2. to evaluate QRNN performance employing part-of-speech (POS) tagging, a sequence labeling task on nine different code-mixed datasets, and 3. to present ablation studies that examine the influence of different data encoding methods, entanglement strategies, and variational circuit depths on model performance.
This novel model shows the potential of QRNN for efficiently labeling input sequences.

objetivo: To address these challenges, a blockchain framework based on a semiquantum key distribution network, referred to as Semi-Quantum Blockchain (SQB), is proposed.
In this paper, we propose a Semi-Quantum Blockchain (SQB) scheme that utilizes one-direction quantum walk on a circle. 1.
In SQB, we propose a Po A consensus mechanism that utilizes one-direction quantum walk on a circular topology.
problema: Quantum attacks represent a significant challenge for blockchain technology. quantum computing disrupts the traditional classical computing paradigm, enabling the rapid resolution of problems that classical computing struggles to address.
Post-quantum cryptography, however, is based on mathematical computational complexity, with its security rely ing on the difficulty of solving mathematical problems.

However, with the proliferation of blockchain applications, several technical and security challenges have emerged, including issues related to transaction speed, scalability, and vulnerabilities in smart contracts [1].
SQB is the first comprehensive semi-quantum blockchain framework, bridging the gap between resource-intensive quantum blockchains and computationally costly postquantum alternatives.
metodo: In the mea surement process, compared with other quantum schemes using QSCD ff problem differentiation algorithm, quantum state comparison techniques and other measure ment methods, this paper’s scheme uses measurement techniques such as single-par ticle measurements and Bell measurements, which are simpler to realize.
SQB employs an enhanced Po A consensus algorithm, maintaining quantum channels only between authority nodes (including the leader node).
Finally, the hash value h M is computed by jointly processing hp and Mh.
The leader node election process is conducted through a quantum voting scheme, which is also based on one-direction quantum walk on a circle [20].
contribuicoes: The security analysis demonstrates that SQB provides robust post-quantum security and significantly outperforms its peers in terms of quantum resource consumption and quantum communication efficiency, establishing it as a secure and efficient blockchain solution.

Despite these conceptual advantages, MBQC’s practical realization of FT and QEC remains less mature than in CBQC, primarily due to the technical challenges associated with generating and maintaining large-scale cluster states with sufficient fidelity and connectivity.

objetivo: This work introduces a complete workflow from first principles for simulating corrosion processes.

We present computational models for aqueous corrosion in magnesium and magnesium-aluminum alloy surf aces, which may be extendable to o t h e rm e t a l l i cs y s t e m s;a n df o rh i gh-temperature oxidation in niobium alloys, which are critical for extreme environments but highly susceptible to degradation.
Results We develop a hybrid classical-quantum workflow to investigate the corrosion mechanisms at the atomic scale in two classes of alloys, under aqueous and high-temperature environments.
problema: However, no single mathematical framework currently exists that can fully capture the complexity of the physical and chemical processes driving corrosion7,8.
Our models for magnesium corrosion are based on nudged elastic band modeling, while our niobium algorithm involves a coupledcluster approximation to map the problem to a classical Ising model.

We conduct a comprehensive resource assessment for quantum modeling of aqueous and high-temperature corrosion processes, marking the first application of the py LIQTR computational tool to a real-world materials science problem.

HER is further complicated by the highly-reactive, and short-lived corrosion intermediates formed during cathodic polarization, presenting a significant challenge in both computational and experiment al investigations of the reaction mechanism.

objetivo: To address this, we propose folding the linear layout into a two-dimensional grid while preserving the efficiency of the linear transformation.
This paper introduces optimal compilation strategies tailored to QFT circuits on the DPQA architecture, addressing these challenges for both linear and grid-like configurations.

Our contributions are threefold: • We propose a Linear Path strategy that achieves the theoretical lower bound in atom-movement count for one-dimensional linear DPQA processors. • We extend this approach to two-dimensional (2D) grid-like DPQA processors through a Zigzag Path strategy, which preserves gate parallelism while minimizing movement overhead. • We demonstrate that our methods outperform state-of-the-art DPQA compilers, including Enola 22 and Atomique23, achieving exponential improvements in movement efficiency and overall circuit fidelity.
problema: However, the all-to-all interaction pattern of QFT poses significant challenges, especially in layouts with limited qubit connectivity.

Despite these strengths, efficiently implementing quantum circuits like the Quantum Fourier Transform (QFT) remains a significant challenge due to atom-movement overheads and connectivity constraints.
However, designing a general-purpose optimal compiler is fundamentally intractable, since optimal quantum circuit compilation is an NP-hard problem.
Hardware constraints such as limited qubit connectivity, short coherence times, and low gate fidelity necessitate optimizations tailored to specific platforms.
metodo: We implement Linear and Zigzag Paths in Python and employ Enola’s Code Gen framework 22 to generate gate execution and schedule movements.
All experiments were conducted on a system running Ubuntu 22.04, equipped with a 40-core Intel Xeon Gold 5215 processor at 2.50 GHz and 512 GB of RAM.
In this section, we evaluate the performance of our proposed methods (called Linear and Zigzag Paths, respectively) for transforming QFT circuits on the DPQA architecture.
Our evaluation aims to demonstrate the effectiveness of our methods in minimizing resource overheads while maintaining high-fidelity quantum operations.

objetivo: This study proposes a framework that uses quantum transfer learning to enhance cybersecurity threat detection by leveraging multiple datasets, including UNSWNB15, CICIDS2017, CSE-CIC-IDS2018, and TON_Io T.

The objective of this research is to investigate the feasibility and potential benefits of integrating quantum transfer learning into intrusion detection systems, thereby bridging the gap between classical machine learning limitations and the emerging capabilities of quantum computing.

The major contributions of this research are as follows: • This study proposes a novel hybrid quantum transfer learning framework that integrates classical pre-trained models with variational quantum circuits for intrusion detection. • Progressive transfer learning is demonstrated across multiple benchmark cybersecurity datasets (UN SW-NB15 → CICIDS2017/CSE-CIC-IDS2018 → TON_Io T), improving model generalization without full retraining.
problema: However, existing quantum-enhanced intrusion detection systems typically employ quantum models only at the classification stage or rely on standalone quantum architectures, while transfer learning-based IDS remain limited to classical-to-classical knowledge reuse.

Despite these advances, classical AI-based models often struggle with highdimensional cybersecurity datasets, heterogeneous feature spaces, and generalization across different network environments13,14.

Quantum transfer learning for cross-domain cybersecurity threat detection and categorization Shtwai Alsubai1, Mohamed Ayari2, Natalia Kryvinska3, Ahmad Almadhor4, Jamel Baili5, Abdullah Al Hejaili6 & Sidra Abbas7 Cybersecurity challenges have become increasingly complex and widespread, and the risks associated with these problems are substantial, affecting thousands of individuals and organisations and being crucial to national security.

The objective of this research is to investigate the feasibility and potential benefits of integrating quantum transfer learning into intrusion detection systems, thereby bridging the gap between classical machine learning limitations and the emerging capabilities of quantum computing.

A data pre-processing pipeline is applied to the dataset.

objetivo: To address these challenges, this research proposes QRGEC: Quantum Reinforcement Learning with Golden Jackal Optimization for Resilient Edge Cloud Coordination in Internet Computing.
The proposed hybrid framework integrates quantum focused policy exploration with adaptive metaheuristic tuning to enhance distributed Internet computing optimization.
Developing methods such as quantum reinforcement learning provides promising opportunities to mitigate these challenges.
problema: However, to the best of our knowledge, no existing work has combined QRL with GJO for enhancing the resilience and coordination efficiency of edge-cloud systems under fluctuating computational and communication constraints.
However, the dynamic heterogeneity within the Internet-scale systems and the stochastic nature of the resources are a major challenge in the effective coordination of the Edge and the cloud layers1.
However, they struggle in environments with changing network topologies and varying resource requirements, thus hindering scalability and dependability in distributed systems.
Outlined networking challenges in distributed quantum computing, emphasizing synchronization, quantum routing, and entanglement-management issues that hinder scalable quantum Internet development19.
metodo: Scope of quantum evaluation The quantum reinforcement learning components in QRGEC are evaluated using state-of-the-art quantum simulation platforms, including Qiskit and Tensor Flow Quantum, integrated with Cloud Sim Plus.

Performance of the proposed QRGEC framework was verified based on hybrid edge cloud systems, implemented using Cloud Sim Plus 6.0 combined with Qiskit 1.2 and Tensor Flow Quantum libraries through extensive simulations.
The quantum reinforcement learning components in QRGEC are implemented using Qiskit and Tensor Flow Quantum simulators.
Training was conducted for E = 200 episodes with (λg,λ q)=(0.3, 0.25) and trust bound δ =0.01.

objetivo: Discover Internet of Things (2026) 6:53 3 Io T-edge coordination layer The Io T-Edge Coordination Layer plays a pivotal role in and this proves optimal task scheduling and resource management across distributed Io T and edge infrastructures. 3.1 Task scheduling framework The goal is to maximize cumulative reward Rt, which balances Qo S, energy efficiency, and throughput.
The main objectives of the work involve the fol lowing: (1) To design a unified AI-driven quantum-resilient Cyber-Physical System (AIQRCPS) framework.
problema: However, as quantum computing and ultra-dense Io T networks tends to get expanded, traditional encrypting data and optimizing systems are insufficient to secure the data in real time.
However, the modern systems struggle with latency management and high computational complexity that occur after quantum computing.
Artificial Intelligence (AI) with post-quantum cryptography and edge intelligence transforms CPS into self-adaptive ecosystems that is capable of handling threats.

Similarly, Elkhodr et al. [27] present the Combined Adaptive Security Framework for Io T (IASF-Io T), which uses AI, blockchain, quantum-resistant cryptog raphy, and Digital Twins for predictive and proactive threat mitigation.
metodo: The simulations are configured with the following parameters to model realistic CPS operation as in Tables 12, 13. 6.4 Performance metrics The performance of the proposed framework is evaluated using the following metrics: Average Latency (ms): It measures the end-to-end delay for task execution across edge-Io T-cloud layers.
At 50 nodes, the proposed framework achieves 74.5 tasks/s, outperforming FOA (58.7 tasks/s), QML (61.8 tasks/s), QHCP (64.1 tasks/s), and CH-DDQ + 2-Brain (65.8 tasks/s).

Experimental evaluation shows that the proposed framework achieves an average latency as low as 7.9-10.7 ms, throughput up to 94.5 tasks/s, energy consumption as low as 3.3-4.8 J/task, and task completion rates exceeding 98% across varying edge/Io T node densities and bandwidths.

Hence, this paper research develops an AI-Driven Quantum-Resilient Cyber-Physical System (AI-QRCPS) framework that combines three novel layers and this includes: (i) a Post-Quantum Security Layer employing lattice-based cryptography and quantum key distribution for end-to-end confidentiality, (ii) an Adaptive Intelligence Layer using Reinforcement Learning (RL) and Firefly Optimization for dynamic resource orchestration and anomaly detection, and (iii) an Io T-Edge Coordination Layer for latency-aware task scheduling using double deep Q-learning.
contribuicoes: The novelty involves a three-layered design, which combines quantum-safe cryptog raphy, adaptive learning-based optimization and Io T-edge coordination for security across heterogeneous CPS environments.
